title: Churn Risk Signals — Leading Indicators
body:
Ranked leading indicators of churn. Note: these are LEADING indicators — they predict future churn even when current usage looks fine. Usage decline is a LAGGING indicator (by the time it shows up, the decision may already be made).

1. EXECUTIVE SPONSOR DEPARTURE — The strongest signal. When the person who championed your product internally leaves, your product loses its internal defender. New leadership often evaluates tools with fresh eyes. Action: Immediately identify and cultivate a new champion before the renewal conversation. Do NOT wait for usage to decline.

2. CHAMPION GOES QUIET — If your day-to-day contact stops responding to check-ins, they may have already mentally moved on. Often precedes usage decline by 4-8 weeks.

3. M&A OR REORG — Acquiring company may consolidate tools. Reorg may move your champion to a different role. Either way, your contract is suddenly under review by people who didn't choose you.

4. INTEGRATION/RELIABILITY ISSUES — Multiple unresolved tickets erode trust. The team starts working around the tool rather than through it.

5. USAGE DECLINE — Lagging indicator. By the time you see this, investigate signals 1-4 for root cause.

RULE OF THUMB: Never assess account health on usage metrics alone. Usage is what already happened. Signals 1-3 tell you what's about to happen.

title: Expansion & Upsell Triggers
body:
Signals that an account is ready for expansion:

1. SEAT SATURATION — >85% of licensed seats active. They're getting value and hitting capacity.

2. FEATURE REQUESTS FOR HIGHER-TIER CAPABILITIES — 'Can we export to Parquet?' 'Do you have SSO?' They're bumping against plan limits.

3. NEW TEAM / BUSINESS UNIT — Organic land-and-expand. If they mention a new BU, get in front of that team early.

4. INTERNAL CHAMPION ADVOCACY — If your champion is socializing your product to other teams unprompted, that's the strongest signal. Give them materials to make the internal pitch.

5. POSITIVE REFERENCE ACTIVITY — Case study participation, speaking at your events, referrals. They're publicly invested in your success.

TIMING: Expansion conversations land best when bundled with renewal. Have the proposal ready 60-90 days before renewal date.

title: Standard Renewal Playbook
body:
T-90 DAYS:
- Run account health review — check usage, tickets, interaction history against churn signals
- Classify: healthy / at-risk / upsell-opportunity
- If at-risk, escalate to CSM for intervention NOW, not at T-30

T-60 DAYS:
- Schedule renewal conversation with economic buyer, not just day-to-day user
- If sponsor changed since last renewal, make sure new sponsor has seen the value story

T-30 DAYS:
- Present renewal terms + expansion proposal if applicable
- Address any open issues — don't go into renewal with unresolved tickets

T-0:
- Close. If you've done T-90 through T-30 right, this is paperwork.

ANTI-PATTERN: Discovering a risk signal at T-30 because nobody ran the T-90 review. At-risk accounts need runway to recover.

title: Competitive Displacement Signals
body:
Signs a competitor is in the account:

- Champion asks detailed questions about features you don't have (they're building a comparison matrix)
- Request for a usage/ROI report 'for internal review' (they're justifying a switch)
- New stakeholder appears who wasn't part of the original buying decision
- Radio silence followed by a sudden flurry of activity near renewal
- Direct mention of evaluating alternatives (at least they're telling you)

If you see these: get the economic buyer on a call, understand what's driving the evaluation, and address it directly. Don't assume loyalty — earn it again.

title: Account Health Classification Rubric
body:
HEALTHY:
- Usage stable or growing
- >70% seat activation
- Active champion who responds to check-ins
- No open P0/P1 tickets
- No leading churn signals

AT-RISK:
- ANY leading churn signal (sponsor departure, champion silence, M&A, reliability issues)
- OR usage decline >20% QoQ
- OR seat activation drop
- OR multiple unresolved tickets

UPSELL-OPPORTUNITY:
- All 'healthy' criteria PLUS one or more expansion triggers (seat saturation, tier-limit requests, new BU, champion advocacy)

IMPORTANT: 'Healthy' and 'at-risk' are not mutually exclusive with usage metrics. An account with growing usage can be at-risk if the sponsor just left. Check leading indicators, not just the dashboard.
